svg.title: Dead-letter queue classification and retry flow for failed agent runs
svg.desc: A failed run is captured, classified as transient or permanent, written to the DLQ, and either replayed with exponential backoff up to a retry cap or held for manual review. Exhausted transient failures are promoted to permanent.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does an agent run wrap, and where is the try/except?')
sendPrompt('Which exception types are caught at the run boundary?')
sendPrompt('What rules classify a failure as transient versus permanent?')
sendPrompt('What counts as transient — timeouts, rate limits, network?')
sendPrompt('What counts as permanent — validation, auth, tool errors?')
sendPrompt('How is exponential backoff scheduled per retry attempt?')
sendPrompt('What does dlq-stats surface for manual review?')
sendPrompt('What triggers promotion from transient to permanent?')
sendPrompt('How is the failure flagged in LangFuse traces?')
